title: Where I Hold Fear Around Taxes and Inheritance
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining the fear that legal systems or taxes will erode the wealth you are building
excerpt: A four-prompt journal set from Day 73 of a 12-week money programme. The prompts move from identifying where the fear around taxes and inheritance sits, toward finding what would change if you fully trusted that your wealth could be protected and passed on in full.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 73 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when avoidance of inheritance planning, tax advice, or legal structures is slowing down decisions that actually matter.

content.text: A four-prompt journal set from Day 73 of a 12-week money programme. Day 73 theme: releasing fear of taxes and inheritance loss. The prompts move from naming where the fear sits, toward finding what changes when you trust that the right structures can protect what you have built. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
Where do you hold fear or resentment around taxes, legal systems, or the idea of money being taken?
Name where it shows up. Is it avoidance of conversations, irritation when it comes up, a low-level dread you push past?
How can you choose to see these structures as neutral and manageable rather than threatening?
Write what changes in the picture when taxes and legal rules are tools you can use rather than forces working against you.
If you fully trusted that everything you create can be preserved and passed on wisely, how would you feel?
Write the feeling in concrete terms. What would you do differently this week?
What trusted resources, people, or advisors can you draw on to help secure your legacy?
Name the specific resources or people already available to you. Write what the next concrete step looks like.
Adapted from the Day 73 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
